<objective>
Install Phase 2 dependencies (motion, react-hook-form, @hookform/resolvers), initialize shadcn/ui with all needed components, extend globals.css with full dark theme + red accent design system, create the delivery preferences Zod schema, build the GHL completion webhook caller (lib/ghl.ts), and implement all 3 new API routes (start, step, complete).

Purpose: Everything in the onboarding UI depends on these building blocks — the components, the dark theme, the API routes for step persistence and completion, and the validation schemas for form data.
Output: Installable shadcn components, dark-themed globals.css, 3 API routes, delivery schema, GHL webhook caller.
</objective>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Install dependencies, initialize shadcn, and set up dark theme</name>
  <files>
    package.json
    components.json
    app/globals.css
    lib/cn.ts
    components/ui/button.tsx
    components/ui/card.tsx
    components/ui/input.tsx
    components/ui/label.tsx
    components/ui/progress.tsx
    components/ui/checkbox.tsx
    components/ui/badge.tsx
    components/ui/separator.tsx
  </files>
  <action>
NOTE: This task lists 12 files but 8 of them (components/ui/*.tsx) are CLI-generated
by `npx shadcn@latest add` — not hand-written code. The effective hand-authored scope
is 4 files (package.json, components.json, app/globals.css, lib/cn.ts). No manual
editing of the 8 shadcn component files is required.

1. Install Phase 2 npm dependencies:
   ```
   npm install motion react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers
   ```

2. Initialize shadcn/ui (this creates components.json and may modify globals.css):
   ```
   npx shadcn@latest init --defaults
   ```
   If shadcn init prompts, accept defaults (New York style, neutral base color, CSS variables: yes).
   shadcn init on Tailwind v4 will create a `lib/utils.ts` or `lib/cn.ts` with the `cn()` utility. Keep whatever it creates.

3. Add all needed shadcn components in one command:
   ```
   npx shadcn@latest add button card input label progress checkbox badge separator
   ```

4. After shadcn init completes, overwrite `app/globals.css` to establish the full dark theme design system. The file must contain:
   - `@import "tailwindcss";` at top
   - Any imports shadcn added (like tw-animate-css) — preserve those
   - `@theme inline` block with CSS variable mappings including `--color-accent: #EF4444;` (red accent per DSGN-01)
   - `:root` block with dark theme variables (this is a dark-only app, forcedTheme="dark" in layout.tsx):
     ```
     --background: #0a0a0a;
     --foreground: #ededed;
     --card: #111111;
     --card-foreground: #ededed;
     --muted: #1a1a1a;
     --muted-foreground: #888888;
     --border: #222222;
     --input: #1a1a1a;
     --ring: #EF4444;
     --primary: #EF4444;
     --primary-foreground: #ffffff;
     --secondary: #1a1a1a;
     --secondary-foreground: #ededed;
     --destructive: #dc2626;
     --destructive-foreground: #ffffff;
     --accent: #1a1a1a;
     --accent-foreground: #ededed;
     --popover: #111111;
     --popover-foreground: #ededed;
     --radius: 0.5rem;
     ```
   - Remove the `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` block — this is always dark
   - Keep the body styles but update font-family to use `var(--font-geist-sans), sans-serif`

IMPORTANT: shadcn init will modify globals.css. Read the file AFTER shadcn init to see what it added, then merge the dark theme variables with shadcn's output. Do NOT blindly overwrite — preserve shadcn's additions.

IMPORTANT: Import motion from "motion/react", NOT "framer-motion".
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd /Users/haseeb/ppl-onboarding && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20 && echo "---" && ls components/ui/ && echo "---" && grep -c "EF4444" app/globals.css</automated>
    <manual>Verify globals.css has dark theme variables and red accent</manual>
  </verify>
  <done>
    - motion, react-hook-form, @hookform/resolvers installed in package.json
    - shadcn initialized with components.json
    - 8 shadcn components installed (button, card, input, label, progress, checkbox, badge, separator)
    - globals.css has full dark theme with #EF4444 red accent
    - No TypeScript errors
  </done>
</task>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 2: Create delivery schema, GHL webhook caller, and 3 API routes</name>
  <files>
    lib/validations/delivery.ts
    lib/ghl.ts
    app/api/brokers/[token]/start/route.ts
    app/api/brokers/[token]/step/route.ts
    app/api/brokers/[token]/complete/route.ts
  </files>
  <action>
1. Create `lib/validations/delivery.ts` — Zod schema for Step 2 delivery preferences:
   ```typescript
   import { z } from 'zod'

   export const DeliveryPrefsSchema = z.object({
     delivery_method: z.enum(['sms', 'email', 'crm_webhook']),
     delivery_email: z.string().email().optional().or(z.literal('')),
     delivery_phone: z.string().optional().or(z.literal('')),
     crm_webhook_url: z.string().url().optional().or(z.literal('')),
     contact_hours: z.enum(['business_hours', 'anytime', 'custom']),
     weekend_pause: z.boolean(),
   })

   export type DeliveryPrefs = z.infer<typeof DeliveryPrefsSchema>
   ```
   Note: `delivery_method` is a SINGLE text column in the DB — radio buttons, not checkboxes.

2. Create `lib/ghl.ts` — GHL completion webhook caller:
   - Import `Broker` type from `@/lib/types`
   - Export async function `notifyGHL(broker: Broker): Promise<void>`
   - Read `GHL_COMPLETION_WEBHOOK_URL` from `process.env` (NOT `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix — server-only)
   - If URL is not configured, log a warning with `console.warn('[ghl] GHL_COMPLETION_WEBHOOK_URL not configured — skipping notification')` and return early
   - POST to the URL with JSON body containing: `ghl_contact_id`, `onboarding_status: 'completed'`, `delivery_preferences` object (delivery_method, delivery_email, delivery_phone, crm_webhook_url, contact_hours, weekend_pause), `updated_fields` object (first_name, last_name, email, phone, company_name)
   - Wrap in try/catch — log errors with `console.error('[ghl]')` prefix but never throw (this runs in waitUntil, throwing would go nowhere)

3. Create `app/api/brokers/[token]/start/route.ts` — POST handler (ONBD-12):
   - Import `createServiceClient` from `@/lib/supabase/server`
   - Extract token from `params: Promise<{ token: string }>`
   - Update broker status to 'in_progress' ONLY where current status is 'not_started' (idempotent — `.eq('status', 'not_started')`)
   - Return `Response.json({ success: true })`

4. Create `app/api/brokers/[token]/step/route.ts` — PATCH handler (ONBD-13):
   - Import `createServiceClient` from `@/lib/supabase/server`
   - Extract token from params, extract `{ step }` from request body
   - Validate step is a number between 1 and 7
   - Update `current_step` on the broker record matching the token
   - Return `Response.json({ success: true })`

5. Create `app/api/brokers/[token]/complete/route.ts` — POST handler (ONBD-10, ONBD-11):
   - Import `waitUntil` from `@vercel/functions`
   - Import `createServiceClient` from `@/lib/supabase/server`
   - Import `notifyGHL` from `@/lib/ghl`
   - Import `Broker` from `@/lib/types`
   - Extract token from params, extract `{ formData }` from request body
   - Update the broker record: set `status: 'completed'`, `completed_at: new Date().toISOString()`, plus all formData fields (first_name, last_name, email, phone, company_name, delivery_method, delivery_email, delivery_phone, crm_webhook_url, contact_hours, weekend_pause)
   - Use `.select().single()` to get the updated broker back
   - If error or no broker, return 500
   - If `process.env.GHL_COMPLETION_WEBHOOK_URL` is set, call `waitUntil(notifyGHL(broker as Broker))`
   - Return `Response.json({ success: true })`
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd /Users/haseeb/ppl-onboarding && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20 && echo "---" && ls app/api/brokers/\[token\]/start/route.ts app/api/brokers/\[token\]/step/route.ts app/api/brokers/\[token\]/complete/route.ts lib/ghl.ts lib/validations/delivery.ts</automated>
    <manual>Verify all 5 files exist and compile without errors</manual>
  </verify>
  <done>
    - lib/validations/delivery.ts exports DeliveryPrefsSchema and DeliveryPrefs type
    - lib/ghl.ts exports notifyGHL with null guard on GHL_COMPLETION_WEBHOOK_URL
    - POST /api/brokers/[token]/start updates status to in_progress (idempotent)
    - PATCH /api/brokers/[token]/step persists current_step (1-7)
    - POST /api/brokers/[token]/complete sets status to completed, stores all form data, fires GHL webhook via waitUntil
    - No TypeScript errors
  </done>
</task>

</tasks>
